commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks (#1134)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks

Indented code blocks (parsed with fence = None) were serialised by
Markdown.ToMd as bare text without any code block markers. When the
output was re-parsed, the content was interpreted as a Paragraph, not
a CodeBlock — breaking the round-trip.

Fix: use triple-backtick fences for code blocks that originally had no
fence, ensuring the serialised form is always a valid fenced code block.
Fenced code blocks round-trip correctly and render identically to
indented code blocks in all Markdown renderers.

Added a round-trip test to cover the case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
Fix font preview updates via visual tree walk

FontFamily is immutable in WinUI 3, so {ThemeResource} bindings cannot
update fonts in-place like SolidColorBrush.Color does for colors.

Solution: walk the visual tree of all preview controls on every theme
change, matching TextBlocks by their Tag attribute (primary/caption/
title/mono) and directly setting FontFamily and FontSize.

Changes:
- Add Tag attributes to all TextBlocks in preview controls (CmdPalPreview,
  DockPreview, ContextMenuPreview, ThemeDesignerPage font preview section)
- Add x:Name=FontPreviewPanel to the font preview StackPanel
- Simplify ApplyFontsToTree to use Tag-only matching (no fragile
  FontFamily.Source string matching)
- Also handle TextBox controls in the visual tree walk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(fabric): Entra ID authentication with Key Vault auto-retrieval (#156)

* docs(copilot): add DuckDB, schema inference, and cache backend details

Add missing public surface entries (duckdb_ext, _schema), document
pluggable CacheBackend architecture, and add focused test suite advice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(provider): integrate Fabric Key Vault authentication into _provider.py

When running in a Microsoft Fabric notebook, _provider.py now auto-detects
the environment via notebookutils.credentials.getSecret capability and uses
ClientSecretCredential backed by Key Vault secrets for Azure OpenAI
bearer-token authentication.

Key design decisions:
- Fabric auth activates only when all required env vars are present
  (AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_APP_CLIENT_ID, KEY_VAULT_URL, KEY_VAULT_SECRET_NAME)
- Falls back to DefaultAzureCredential when Fabric vars are missing
- OPENAI_API_KEY and AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY still take priority over Fabric auth
- Logs INFO-level env var status on Fabric detection for notebook UX
- Error messages include Fabric-specific guidance when in Fabric

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(provider): extract Fabric auth logic to _fabric module

Move all Fabric-specific functions (_is_fabric_environment,
_fabric_auth_configured, _log_fabric_environment_info,
_build_fabric_token_provider, and credentials error section)
from _provider.py into _fabric.py. Provider now delegates to
fabric.* calls, keeping _provider.py focused on DI registration.

Update test imports and @patch paths from openaivec._provider.*
to openaivec._fabric.* accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(fabric): add detailed auth flow guidance for incomplete configuration

When Fabric is detected with partially-set env vars, emit a UserWarning
with the full authentication flow explanation (Workspace → Key Vault →
Service Principal → Foundry AI User role), per-variable status with
examples, step-by-step setup instructions, and a note about
set_default_registrations() for late configuration.

Error messages and build_token_provider ValueError also now include the
auth flow guide and setup steps for maximum discoverability.

New functions: is_partially_configured(), warn_incomplete_configuration()
Updated: log_environment_info(), build_credentials_error_section(),
build_token_provider() error message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(auth): switch to DefaultAzureCredential + env vars for Entra ID auth

- Remove custom build_token_provider() and ClientSecretCredential from _fabric.py
- Always use DefaultAzureCredential (EnvironmentCredential detects SP vars)
- Add AzureTenantID, AzureClientID, AzureClientSecret ValueObjects in _model.py
- Register new ValueObjects in DI container (_provider.py)
- Remove Fabric-specific BearerTokenProvider branch; always register DAC path
- Update REQUIRED_VARS to standard Azure SDK env vars (AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET)
- Update all guidance messages for new env var approach
- Add setup_entra_id() in spark_ext.py for Spark + Service Principal auth
- Update tests for new architecture (51 provider tests pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(fabric): auto-retrieve client secret from Key Vault on Fabric driver

On Fabric environments, automatically retrieve the Service Principal client
secret from Azure Key Vault using notebookutils.credentials.getSecret().
This eliminates the need to manually set AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET.

Two auth paths are now supported:
- KV path: AZURE_TENANT_ID + AZURE_CLIENT_ID + KEY_VAULT_URL + KEY_VAULT_SECRET_NAME
- Direct path: AZURE_TENANT_ID + AZURE_CLIENT_ID + AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET

Changes:
- _fabric.py: add retrieve_client_secret(), dual-path is_auth_configured(),
  updated guidance messages with detailed setup steps
- _provider.py: call fabric.retrieve_client_secret() during registration
- spark_ext.py: setup_entra_id() now accepts kv_url/kv_secret_name as
  alternative to client_secret for KV retrieval on Fabric driver
- Tests: 58 provider tests covering both auth paths, KV retrieval,
  error messages, warnings, and logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(provider): move KV retrieval into AzureClientSecret provider

Also add KeyVaultURL and KeyVaultSecretName ValueObjects to _model.py
and register them via DI, consistent with the existing pattern.

commit.message:
feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service — KG-backed urgency + resolution (ENG-03) (#143)

* feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service (ENG-03)

Implement ForeshadowingTracker — a backend service that queries KG entities
of type 'foreshadowing' and provides urgency-sorted listing + resolution
capability.

Key decisions:
- Pure SQLite queries (INV-4: Memory-First, zero LLM calls)
- resolve() is a structured data operation (attributes_json flag toggle),
  NOT a Skill invocation (INV-6 compliant)
- Deterministic clock injection (P4) — no Date.now() in logic
- 30s TTL cache consistent with storyStatusService
- Graceful degradation: returns [] until kg_entities CHECK constraint is
  extended to include 'foreshadowing' type (migration 0013 gap)
- Imports DbLike/DbStatement from storyStatusService (shared DB abstraction)

Test coverage: 37 tests using in-memory SQLite (real SQL queries, not mocks):
- listActive: empty project, single/multiple items, urgency sort, resolved
  exclusion, project isolation, type filtering
- resolve: success, non-existent, wrong project, already-resolved, attribute
  preservation, cache auto-invalidation
- openDays/urgency: exact days, partial days, today
- firstChapterHint: present, absent, non-string, empty attributes
- Cache: TTL (30s), boundary (29999ms vs 30000ms), invalidation, per-project
- Dispose: listActive throws, resolve throws, cache cleared

Closes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engagement): align foreshadowing tracker with KG schema + spec urgency model

R1 audit findings addressed:
- ISO timestamp parsing: created_at is TEXT in production (kgCoreService
  writes new Date().toISOString()), now parsed via new Date(str).getTime()
- resolve() writes ISO string for updated_at (matches KG convention)
- urgency field: clamped to [0, 1] range via min(1.0, openDays / 30),
  with @deviation JSDoc documenting chapter-based spec vs time-based impl
- Test schema: created_at/updated_at changed from INTEGER to TEXT to match
  production kg_entities schema
- URGENCY_DECAY_DAYS = 30 ≈ 10 chapters at ~3 days/chapter writing pace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix CORS and per-group readiness for multi-channel replay

- Add CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) to WebSocketHost
  so frontend on different port can read /health endpoint
- Add AnyGroupReady event to MultiFeedManager, fired when first group
  reaches RealTime (instead of waiting for all groups)

[Repo Assist] perf: compile regex instances to module-level singletons (#1162)

* perf: compile regex instances to module-level singletons

Five locations were creating a new uncompiled Regex on every call:

- PageContentList.mkPageContentMenu: new Regex per page rendered
- Formatting.fs (search index): Regex.Replace (static, uncompiled) per HTML page

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves tight list formatting (#1157)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves tight list formatting

Tight lists (no blank lines between items in source) were converted to
loose lists by ToMd because the list serialiser always emitted a blank
line after every item.

Root cause: the Unordered and Ordered ListBlock arms in formatParagraph
unconditionally yielded "" after each item, regardless of whether the
list was tight or loose.

Fix: detect a tight list (every item is a single Span paragraph, as
produced by the parser for simple/tight items) and suppress inter-item
blank lines. A single trailing blank line is still emitted after the
entire list so the document structure is preserved.

Added three tests:
- tight unordered list round-trip (no blank lines)
- tight ordered list round-trip (no blank lines)
- loose list round-trip (items with blank lines preserved)

All 284 Markdown tests pass.

feat: アダプティブスロットリング改善・ダッシュボード機能追加 (#134)

* fix: Phase C（フォルダ先行作成）専用の AdaptiveConcurrencyController を生成するよう修正

Phase C と Phase D が同じコントローラーを共有していたため、フォルダ作成中の 429 が
ファイル転送用コントローラーの CurrentDegree を下げ、Phase D 開始時の並列度に影響していた。
また「並列度を 46 → 45/50 に削減」のようにフォルダ作成フェーズで MaxParallelTransfers
ベースの値がログに出力される問題があった。

修正内容:
- SharePointMigrationPipeline に _folderCreationController フィールドを追加し、
  Phase C ではこちらを優先使用（null の場合は _concurrencyController にフォールバック）
- App.xaml.cs / TransferCommand.cs で maxDegree = MaxParallelFolderCreations の
  専用コントローラーを生成してパイプラインに渡すよう変更
- 各呼び出し元で folderCreationController を適切に Dispose

* fix: Phase C/D でフェーズに応じて 429 通知先コントローラーを切り替えるよう修正

前回の修正（b9f0660）では folderCreationController を生成したが、onRateLimit コールバックが
転送用 concurrencyController に固定されたままだったため、Phase C の 429 がフォルダ作成用
コントローラーに届かず並列度制御が効かない問題があった。

- SharePointMigrationPipeline に activateController コールバックを追加。
  Phase C 開始時に folderCreationController、Phase D 開始時に concurrencyController を
  アクティブ化することで onRateLimit の通知先をフェーズごとに切り替える
- App.xaml.cs: onRateLimit を Volatile フィールド経由のプロキシに変更し、
  activateController で通知先を動的に切り替えるよう修正
- CliServices.cs: SetActiveController メソッドを追加（任意のコントローラーを直接アクティブ化）
- TransferCommand.cs: activateController コールバックをパイプラインに渡すよう修正

これにより Phase C は 1〜MaxParallelFolderCreations(16)、
Phase D は 1〜MaxParallelTransfers(50) でそれぞれ独立して動的並列度制御される

* test: AdaptiveConcurrencyController テストを decreaseMultiplier 動作に合わせて修正

429 減速ロジックを -1（固定）から *0.5（乗数）に変更したことに伴い、
8 件のユニットテストの期待値を新しい動作に合わせて更新。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* fix: ToMd now serialises single-line equation blocks as $$...$$ not \begin{equation}

Markdown.ToMd previously serialised LatexBlock paragraphs (i.e. those
written as `$$body$$` or `$$$...$$$`) using the LaTeX environment form:

    \begin{equation}
    body
    \end{equation}

This broke the round-trip for the common `$$...$$` notation and caused
the 'ToMd preserves LaTeX display math' test to fail.

Fix: when the environment is 'equation' and the body is a single line,
emit the compact `$$body$$` form instead. Multi-line bodies and
non-equation environments keep the `\begin{env}...\end{env}` form.

All 295 markdown tests pass.

commit.message:
fix(ci): disable elevation in CI GraphHopper config to avoid slow SRTM downloads

SRTM elevation tile downloads from NASA were causing the graph build
to exceed 10 minutes in CI. Elevation is irrelevant to E2E test
correctness (tests check routing works, not slope penalties).

Changes:
- infra/graphhopper/config-ci.yml: new CI-only config with elevation
  disabled and average_slope removed from encoded values
- ci.yml: copy config-ci.yml over config.yml early in the e2e-tests
  job (before the cache step) so both the pre-build container and the
  Aspire test host use a consistent, compatible config
- Cache key bumped to iow-v2 to discard the old elevation-based graph

Graph build time should drop from 10+ min to ~2-3 min on cold runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix member template variadic pack expansion in constructor calls

- Add variadic pack parameter handling to instantiate_member_function_template_core
  to expand Args... parameters into individual args_0, args_1, etc. parameters
- Detect variadic template parameters by checking if the parameter type name
  matches a variadic template parameter (handles cases where is_parameter_pack
  flag isn't set)
- Set up pack_param_info_ for body parsing to enable pack expansion
- Add pack expansion support in constructor argument parsing
  (parse_expression followed by ...) to expand args... into individual args
- Restore pack_param_info_ at function end to avoid polluting outer context

This enables member function templates with variadic parameters like:
  template <typename... Args>
  void emplace(Args&&... args) { value = T(args...); }
to be properly instantiated and have their bodies parsed correctly.
